finding: on mobile/native-scroll (virtualize=false) messageListHandle.scrollToBottom() is a no-op on the plain path, and the effect had no messagesEndRef.scrollIntoView fallback, so a pending approval is never brought into view on mobile — the exact case the fix targets

the new approval effect omits the sibling auto-follow's !hasMoreAfter guard, so an approval added while the window is anchored mid-conversation (after an around= jump) makes scrollToBottom jump to the loaded-slice tail and re-enter the bottom-load sentinel, cascading loadNewerMessages

the approval effect lacks the !pendingAnchorRef.current guard, so an approval during an in-flight older-page prepend races the restoreAnchor layout-effect and can produce a janky double scroll while reading history

toolCalls is a singleton Map never cleared on conversation switch (clearToolCalls has zero call sites) with no conversation id, so on a fresh ConversationPage mount a leftover pending_approval from a different conversation yanks the newly-opened one to the bottom

if an approval's toolCalls mutation lands before the initialScrollConvIdRef latch is set (approval before messages.size>0), the effect early-returns and never re-fires for it (latch is a ref, messages not a dep) — an approval could be silently missed
fires exactly once per tool_use_id (deduped via seen Set) and does not fire on pending_approval→started or progress transitions — the once-per-approval accounting is correct

subscribing ConversationPage to Stores.McpComposer.toolCalls makes this large top-level page (and the non-memoized MessageList) re-render on EVERY tool-call mutation, including per-tick setToolCallProgress during downloads — a new render source
the effect re-scans the whole toolCalls Map on every change and scrolledApprovalsRef grows for the mount lifetime, but both are bounded by the small number of tool calls — negligible

reading toolCalls (immer Map with enableMapSet) as an effect dep works: every add/updateToolCall runs in a set() producer so immer yields a new Map ref, so the [toolCalls] dep changes and the subscription cannot miss a mutation; the ToolCallPendingApprovalContent removal (containerRef/scrolledApprovals/effect) is fully cleaned up (imports trimmed to useState)

scrolledApprovalsRef Set is never pruned/cleared across conversation switches (intentionally persists so A→B→A does not re-scroll), so it accumulates one UUID per approval ever seen — a minor unbounded-growth smell; ref (not state) is correct to avoid re-render churn

mcpApprovalRequired mutates two stores (McpComposer + Chat streamingMessage) so the injected approval row may be unmeasured when scrollToBottom fires; MessageList.scrollToBottom mitigates via startReassert (3 RAF frames), but the cap can under-shoot if the card height settles slowly
StrictMode double-invoke is safe: the first invoke adds the id to seen + scrolls, the synchronous second finds it in seen and does not scroll again

scrollToBottom moves the viewport but neither moves focus to the approval nor emits an aria-live announcement, so SR/keyboard users are not notified an action-required prompt appeared (the removed scrollIntoView had the same gap — not a regression, but a missed opportunity)
removing the reduced-motion-aware scrollIntoView is a11y-neutral-to-positive: the replacement virtualizer scrollToIndex is an instant jump that respects prefers-reduced-motion by default

the reactive read const { toolCalls } = Stores.McpComposer and the imperative messageListRef.scrollToBottom() are both idiomatic (read matches McpToolUseRenderer; scrollToBottom is the sanctioned handle for the virtualized viewport)

the new effect diverges from its sibling auto-follow effect by dropping the pendingAnchorRef/hasMoreAfter/isAtBottom guards; the isAtBottom bypass is intentional+documented, but silently dropping the anchor/hasMoreAfter guards on the SAME scroll surface should be explicit, not an omission

the test asserts the approval IS in-viewport but never asserts it was OUT of viewport first ('below the fold' inferred only from the jump button), so a virtualizer that lands the tail in view on reload could false-green without exercising the fix

the second mockGetMessages re-registration DOES take effect (Playwright runs the last-registered route first) and no scroll-on-send exists in ChatInput/Chat.store, so the fix is genuinely required for the test to pass — a real improvement over the old scrollIntoView spy; #134 wrapping tests unchanged and still meaningful
call.status === 'pending_approval' correctly matches the store-local McpToolCall union (started|pending_approval|completed|error), NOT the OpenAPI McpToolCall — the right type is used
scrolledApprovalsRef and hasNewApproval are clear and consistent with the removed module-level scrolledApprovals Set — no naming issue
no crash risk: messageListRef.current?.scrollToBottom() is null-guarded, toolCalls is always an initialized Map, conversation?.id is optional-chained, Map iteration + call.status access are safe
